purpose: Defines the `!p_track_resume [--dashboard {id}]` command — comprehensive resume of a stalled/interrupted !p_track swarm. The most complete 'pick up where we left off' command. Phase 0 reads ALL 7 master instruction docs (NON-NEGOTIABLE). Phase 1 reconstructs state from disk (initialization.json, all progress, logs, master_state, plan, project context, rebuilds upstream cache). Phase 2 classifies each task and detects agent health via 10/20-minute thresholds (alive vs stale vs uncertain). Phase 3 cleans up stale progress files, builds full p_track_v2 worker prompts with PREVIOUS ATTEMPT/ROOT CAUSE/REMEDIATION sections, dispatches all ready. Phase 4 runs the standard p_track execution loop. Phase 5 delivers the NON-NEGOTIABLE final report with Resume Details section.
gotchas: Do NOT blindly re-dispatch all in-progress tasks — check progress file recency first; clobbering a running worker wastes computation and risks file conflicts
Agent health classification thresholds: 10-min for recent activity = alive; >20-min started_at with no updates = stale; reading_context/planning >10-min = stale
If plan.json is missing (legacy swarm), the master MUST regenerate it with a fresh deep-thinking pass BEFORE re-dispatching — hooks block dispatch otherwise
Progress files are AUTHORITATIVE over master_state.json when they conflict

Reading 7 specific files in Phase 0 is non-negotiable — CLAUDE.md, tracker_master_instructions.md, dashboard_writes.md, eager_dispatch.md, worker_prompts.md, compaction_recovery.md, failure_recovery.md
Worker prompt MUST use TEMPLATE_VERSION: p_track_v2 — workers include this in their progress file template_version field

RESUME CONTEXT section in worker prompts must instruct workers to check actual file state — files may have been modified by completed upstream tasks or partially modified by an interrupted previous worker
Likely-alive monitored agents that stop updating for 10+ minutes get reclassified as stale and re-dispatched
Final report Resume Details section is REQUIRED and must cover: tasks completed before resume, re-dispatched (stale), retried (failed), dispatched fresh, agents found alive
patterns: Six phases: Phase 0 Read Core Instructions → Phase 1 State Reconstruction → Phase 2 Health Check+Classification → Phase 3 Cleanup+Dispatch+Monitor → Phase 4 Execution Loop → Phase 5 Completion+Report
Agent health Alive vs Stale indicators (3 alive, 4 stale signals; ANY alive = treat as alive, ALL stale = treat as stale)

Worker prompt sections in fixed order: TASK → DESCRIPTION → CONTEXT → PROJECT/TRACKER ROOT → CONVENTIONS → REFERENCE CODE → UPSTREAM RESULTS → SIBLING TASKS → CRITICAL → SUCCESS CRITERIA → FILES → DIRECTORY → RESUME CONTEXT → PREVIOUS ATTEMPT (if retry) → PREPARATION → PROGRESS REPORTING → DEVIATION REPORTING → EXECUTION RULES → RETURN FORMAT
Dispatch sequence: launch agent FIRST → log AFTER dispatch → write master_state checkpoint after batch
Compaction recovery: detect by losing cached upstream results → read master_state → read initialization → read all progress (authoritative) → cross-reference + rebuild cache → log → resume
conventions: Verification gate at end of Phase 0
Quick Reference table comparing !p_track_resume vs !track_resume vs !dispatch --ready vs !retry vs !resume
Final Rules section with 14 non-negotiable rules grouped by Instruction Reading, Agent Health, Dispatch+Tracking, State Management, Completion
